name: EGC
description: Local MCP runtime that gives persistent cross-session memory to 20 AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, Amp, Kiro, VS Code Copilot, JetBrains Junie, and more). SQLite-backed state survives context resets, a user-wide global scope shares context across every project, and a session bus lets parallel sessions split territory with cooperative path locks. Bidirectional sync keeps all tool config files in sync. Install: npm install -g @egchq/egc

tools.name: get_state
tools.description: Returns the current project memory: decisions made, preferences established, things to avoid, and what to pick up next. State is scoped to the current git branch, with a deduplicated user-wide Global Memory section appended when it exists. Call this at the START of every session to restore context.

tools.name: update_state
tools.description: Updates the project memory with decisions made this session. Writes to the state file of the current git branch, or to the user-wide global memory with scope 'global'. Call this at the END of every session. Merges with existing state: does not erase previous memory.

tools.name: session_announce
tools.description: Announce this session on the session bus: registers presence with an optional territory and doubles as heartbeat. Returns live peer sessions so parallel sessions split territory instead of colliding.

tools.name: claim_path
tools.description: Cooperatively lock a path before editing it. Fail-fast: a conflicting live lock is refused with holder info instead of queued. Locks are TTL-bounded and die with their session.

tools.name: release_path
tools.description: Release a path lock held by this session. Only the holder can release.

tools.name: session_peers
tools.description: List live sessions and active path locks on the session bus. Use before starting parallel work.

tools.name: get_project_state
tools.description: Returns server health metadata: storage engine and write arbitration mode. Use this to verify the egc-memory server is running before calling get_state or update_state.

tools.name: store_decision
tools.description: Persist a single decision to SQLite with write-lock arbitration to prevent concurrent conflicts. Decisions are queryable via query_history and surfaced in get_state.

tools.name: query_history
tools.description: Return a paginated list of past decisions stored in SQLite. Each entry includes decision text, context label, and timestamp. Use limit and offset for pagination.

tools.name: search_history
tools.description: Keyword search over decision history with BM25 relevance ranking (SQLite FTS5). Returns decisions ordered by relevance with scores normalized to [0, 1].

tools.name: working_memory_set
tools.description: Store a transient key-value entry scoped to the current project. Expires automatically after ttl_seconds. Use for debug flags, temporary task context, or values that should not pollute long-term state.

tools.name: working_memory_get
tools.description: Retrieve a single transient entry by key for the current project. Returns null if the entry does not exist or has expired.

tools.name: working_memory_list
tools.description: List all live transient entries for the current project, ordered by key. Expired entries are excluded.

tools.name: lesson_save
tools.description: Persist a lesson learned during this session with a confidence score. Lessons decay over time when not reinforced. Use to record patterns, rules, or observations the AI should remember across sessions.

tools.name: lesson_recall
tools.description: Search active lessons above a confidence threshold. Lessons below 0.2 confidence are archived. Returns lessons ranked by confidence score.

tools.name: lesson_reinforce
tools.description: Reinforce an existing lesson when the same pattern is observed again. Increases confidence by 0.15 (capped at 1.0). Call when a previously stored lesson proves relevant.

tools.name: detect_patterns
tools.description: Analyze captured runtime events and surface recurring behaviors across sessions. Detects repeated commands and recurring errors using frequency analysis. Returns patterns with type, occurrence count, and an actionable suggestion.

tools.name: compress_observations
tools.description: Compress recent raw hook observations into structured typed summaries to reduce token usage for context injection. Returns count and summary of compressed items.
